reflection_summary: The agent achieves high episode reward (mean ~2893) by exploiting shaping components, particularly r_approach_region (dominant at 46.7%), without ever achieving safe contact or stable landing. Episode length is always 1000, indicating indefinite hovering. True task success is 0. This is a clear case of reward hacking due to shaping-goal mismatch.

observed_facts: r_approach_region is dominant component (46.7% of total sum)
no terminal rewards paid in any episode
likely_false_positives: component_dominance flag (46.7% is below strict 50% threshold but still problematic)
root_cause_hypotheses: Dense shaping components (especially r_approach_region) provide high reward for staying in the approach region without progressing to landing; terminal bonuses are too small relative to accumulated shaping reward, so the agent has no incentive to land.

strategy.why_atomic_or_separable: Decreasing r_approach_region without simultaneously increasing the terminal bonus would remove the main reward signal and likely cause learning collapse; the two changes must be coordinated to shift incentive towards landing while preserving gradient.
editor_constraints: Do not remove r_approach_region entirely; maintain some approach shaping to guide early learning.
Significantly increase the weight of r_stable_landing_once (or reduce its duration condition) so that terminal success becomes attractive.
Consider adding a small per-step penalty or a bonus for early termination to discourage indefinite hovering.
must_preserve: r_energy penalty (keep small to penalize unnecessary firing)
must_avoid: Making the terminal bonus so large that the episode reward becomes sparse and prevents bootstrapping.
Over-penalizing energy to the point the agent never fires engines and cannot learn.
strategy.expected_effect: The agent should learn to land successfully, increasing success rate; reward mean may drop initially but stabilize once landings occur; shaping components still guide progress but no longer dominate.
strategy.risk_analysis: If the terminal bonus is set too high but remains hard to reach, the agent may not receive enough reward to bootstrap. If the approach component is removed entirely, the agent may wander aimlessly. A gradual transfer of weight may be safer.

package_should_be_rejected_if: The edit package reduces total reward signal so much that the agent fails to learn any approach behavior.
Success rate remains 0 after training with the new reward.
package_should_be_accepted_if: The agent starts achieving safe contact and stable landing within a reasonable number of training steps.
Shaping components still provide a useful gradient but no longer dominate episode reward.
safety_notes: Monitor episode length after edit; continued max-length episodes indicate insufficient terminal incentive or too much shaping.
